summary: Matrix recording of a nostalgic first Dead concert, highlighted by the unforgettable opening of Mississippi Half‑Step, though the performance isn’t considered a classic.
review: The reviewer’s emotional connection makes this show stand out – it was their very first Grateful Dead experience and the opening notes of "Mississippi Half‑Step" are etched in memory. The matrix source (mtx) provides a listenable capture, but no specific audio flaws or highs are noted, and the performance is described as not quite up to classic‑Dead standards.

ai_rating.rationale: Only one detailed five‑star review; emotional enthusiasm raises the rating, but lack of concrete recording or performance details keeps confidence low.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No explicit comments on sound clarity; reviewer thanks the tape archivists (Fanger, Rudy), implying an acceptable matrix capture.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Personal, nostalgic flow – highlighted by the opening of Mississippi Half‑Step; no detailed critique of pacing.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mentioned that Jerry was "sadly on his way out", indicating health concerns rather than performance praise.
